feedback.text: I would like to say what I would like to say, unless I have lost that, the company is not supposed to have the opportunity to have games for which you have paid a lot of money (in-game or physical), not simply to cancel and disappear. Especially in the era of Technology, EA has already proved that the products have been discontinued and have simply been removed from the libraries by other users. If, businesses want games to get the ‘mandatory’ online and make it easier for their users to access with systems such as ‘Private Servers’ via Pier-To-Pier systems. They are happy to write this in their EULA, who are not liable when users do something, as long as the product is not simply stolen from us. Otherwise, they should pay the money back to all users, as switching off a service is only legitimate if there is also a time window, such as for 24h test phases.
